description: Repository-level checks for Anthropic-style skill packaging and Claude Code-ready skill metadata.

cases.prompt: Audit the portable skills directory for valid Anthropic-style skill packages.
assertions: Every immediate child directory under skills/ contains SKILL.md.
Each SKILL.md begins with YAML frontmatter.
Each frontmatter block contains name and description.
Portable frontmatter uses only name, description, tools, and optional compatibility fields.
The frontmatter name matches the skill directory name.
Skill names are 64 characters or fewer, use only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens, and avoid reserved words.
Skill names and descriptions do not contain XML tags.
Descriptions are 100 words or fewer, 1024 characters or fewer, and describe what the skill does and when to trigger it.
Descriptions explain when the skill triggers.
SKILL.md contains Markdown instructions after frontmatter, starting with an H1 heading and at least one H2 section.

cases.prompt: Audit the Claude Code skill directory against the portable skills directory.
assertions: .claude/skills and skills contain the same immediate skill directory names.
Claude Code skill metadata uses the current certificate-skills MCP server prefix.
Claude Code skill frontmatter uses allowed-tools and portable skill frontmatter uses tools.
Claude Code skill frontmatter uses only name, description, allowed-tools, and optional compatibility fields.
Portable skill tools match the corresponding Claude Code allowed-tools after removing the MCP prefix.
Claude Code skill prompts include When to Use, When NOT to Use, Instructions, and Anti-Patterns sections.
No legacy MCP server-name references remain.

cases.prompt: Audit skill package layout for progressive disclosure.
assertions: evals/evals.json uses the skill-creator eval shape with skill_name and evals.
Skill eval manifests use only the official skill_name and evals top-level fields.
Skill eval cases use only id, prompt, expected_output, files, and expectations fields with the expected JSON types.
Every portable skill contains evals/evals.json with skill_name matching the skill directory and 2-3 eval cases.
Skill eval directories contain only evals.json and optional files/ fixtures.
Skill eval files entries, when present, are relative paths under evals/files/ that resolve inside the skill root.
evals/skills-structure.json contains repository structure checks.
scripts/skill_validation.py contains shared repository and package validation logic.
scripts/validate-skills.sh delegates to the shared Python validator.
scripts/package-skills.py can package portable skill directories into .skill archives.
scripts/package-skills.py reuses the shared validator before writing archives.
.gitignore keeps generated .skill archives, skill eval workspaces, and dist/ outputs ignored.
Generated .skill archives and skill eval workspaces are not tracked as source files.
SKILL.md files remain lightweight.
Bundled resources live in supported skill-creator directories: references/, scripts/, assets/, or evals/.
Reusable detailed material lives in references/ when present.
Relative links from SKILL.md resolve to files inside the same skill package.
Every file under a skill references/ directory is linked from that skill's SKILL.md.
SKILL.md reference links explain when to read the referenced file.
Reference files over 300 lines include a Contents or Table of Contents heading.
Every file under skill scripts/ and assets/ directories is linked from that skill's SKILL.md.
Files under skill scripts/ directories are executable.
Skill package content rejects high-risk shell patterns that could compromise the user's system.
Skill directory and frontmatter names are lowercase hyphenated.
Portable SKILL.md files keep When to Use trigger guidance in frontmatter descriptions instead of body sections.
Portable skills do not duplicate repository installation instructions.
Portable skill descriptions match the trigger-oriented Claude Code skill descriptions.
